title: netavark: unable to append rule '! -d ff00::/8 -j MASQUERADE' to table 'nat'
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

kind create cluster fails due to a netavark issue that I do not understand very clearly.

Some details:
* I am on a corporate network with a proxy
* Using a rootful podman machine
* I had to set a custom resolv.conf due to our network dns (podman can pull down images now)
* I added a root ca trust to podman machine


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. be on my corporate network (the only way to get to the outside is through a proxy)
2. run kind create cluster



**Describe the results you received:**

kind fails to create a basic network.  If i remove "--net kind" from the command in the error message then that command completes with no error.  So the error scope seems to be with the network.

The error
```bash
λ kind create cluster
enabling experimental podman provider
Creating cluster "kind" ...
 • Ensuring node image (kindest/node:v1.25.2) 🖼  ...
 ✓ Ensuring node image (kindest/node:v1.25.2) 🖼
 • Preparing nodes 📦   ...
 ✗ Preparing nodes 📦 
ERROR: failed to create cluster: command "podman run --name kind-control-plane --hostname kind-control-plane --label io.x-k8s.kind.role=control-plane --privileged --tmpfs /tmp --tmpfs /run --volume 957df470e84b1edef79cc743e4ac54c42760c13c5ed5297258ad34c8b0c214a0:/var:suid,exec,dev --volume /lib/modules:/lib/modules:ro -e KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_CONTAINERD_SNAPSHOTTER 
--detach --tty --net kind --label io.x-k8s.kind.cluster=kind -e container=podman -e HTTPS_PROXY=http://my.corp.proxy.net:8080/ -e https_proxy=http://my.corp.proxy.net:8080/ -e 'NO_PROXY=fc00:f853:ccd:e793::/64,10.89.0.0/24,localhost,127.0.0.1,afewotheraddresses.com,host.docker.internal,host.container.internal,10.96.0.0/16,10.244.0.0/16,kind-control-plane,.svc,.svc.cluster,.svc.cluster.local' -e 'no_proxy=fc00:f853:ccd:e793::/64,10.89.0.0/24,localhost,127.0.0.1,afewotheraddresses.com,host.docker.internal,host.container.internal,10.96.0.0/16,10.244.0.0/16,kind-control-plane,.svc,.svc.cluster,.svc.cluster.local' -e HTTP_PROXY=http://my.corp.proxy.net:8080/ -e http_proxy=http://my.corp.proxy.net:8080/ --publish=127.0.0.1:51681:6443/tcp -e KUBECONFIG=/etc/kubernetes/admin.conf docker.io/kindest/node@sha256:9be91e9e9cdf116809841fc77ebdb8845443c4c72fe5218f3ae9eb57fdb4bace" failed with error: exit status 127

Command Output: Error: netavark (exit code 1): unable to append rule '! -d ff00::/8 -j MASQUERADE' to table 'nat': code: 2, msg: ip6tables v1.8.7 (legacy): Couldn't load target `MASQUERADE':No such file or directory

Try `ip6tables -h' or 'ip6tables --help' for more information.
```

the network info:

```bash
λ podman network inspect kind
[
     {
          "name": "kind",
          "id": "3f08d6d202bb19911c2f214e43fca44d10e307b1b08a4a9b482faa40e3723d32",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman1",

```


**Describe the results you expected:**

kind creates a basic cluster

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

```bash
λ kind version
kind v0.16.0 go1.19.1 windows/amd64
```

this is podman on windows

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes 
maybe I don't know what I should be looking for though??

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
windows 10 21H2 Build 19044.2006

comments.body:
Any more details about the environment Kind is running here? Is that command running straight on the VM, in another container?

Any chance the `ip6table_nat` module is not loaded?
I tried it in PowerShell and cmder.  I didn't install kind on the podman machine WSL vm it's in windows.  How do I troubleshoot the ip6table_nat module?

Oh, this is WSL2, not a VM.

@n1hility Any idea if WSL2 supports v6 and/or loads the IPv6 NAT kernel modules?

@mheon yes its compiled in:
```
zcat /proc/config.gz | grep IP6_NF_NAT
CONFIG_IP6_NF_NAT=y
```

Not sure why the masq rule / load fails. I will try to reproduce

@JasonCubic Ah looks like you are using WSL 1 -  kernel: 4.19.128-microsoft-standard

WSL1 is deprecated and only supports partial emulation of linux system calls (podman requires WSL2)

Try switching to WSL2 like so:
```
podman machine stop
wsl --set-version podman-machine-default 2
wsl --shutdown
podman machine start
```

Depending on the state of your instance you might need to flip the default and recreate it
```
podman machine stop
wsl --set-default-version 2
wsl --shutdown
podman machine rm
podman machine init
podman machine start
```

hmm I wonder what's going on there.  I am using WSLv2 as default.

```powershell
> Get-ItemPropertyValue `
      -Path HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Lxss `
      -Name DefaultVersion
2
```

```bash
λ wsl -l -v
  NAME                      STATE           VERSION
* Ubuntu-22.04              Stopped         2
  Ubuntu-20.04              Stopped         2
  podman-machine-default    Running         2
  Ubuntu-18.04              Stopped         2
  Debian                    Stopped         2
```

I stopped and rm'd the podman machine a few times and it always comes back to 4.19.128-microsoft-standard.  I even toggled the wsl default to 1 and then back to 2.  

I have a win 11 box at home and sure enough the kernel on its `podman info` is WSL2.  

Could there be a bug with podman and my version 10 of windows?  Is there a way to force the newer kernel?

@n1hility Good news!  My problem is resolved.  My corp manages my os updates, but I have local admin so I was able to fix this one.  My corp had not sent out any updates to the WSL kernel.  I was using WSLv2 but, even with WSLv2 the older WSL kernel version seems to have been the issue.

If anyone else runs into this here is how I solved it:
- Was able to see the older kernel in WSL by running: `wsl --status`
- I stopped podman machine and rm'd it
- I stopped all running WSL distro's using: `wsl --shutdown`
- Went and got a WSL kernel update here:  <https://www.catalog.update.microsoft.com/Search.aspx?q=wsl>
- I clicked to dowload the newest cab for my architecture x64
- I opened that cab file using 7-zip and extracted the file: wsl_update_x64.msi
- I ran this msi and went through an installer wizard (basically just pressed OK once, there were no options to choose)
- when I ran `wsl --status` the kernel was the newer updated version
- recreated and started podman machine

and kind works now.  :-)

This one is solved, it was my WSL kernel version.  Thank you for the help.  

UPDATE: use `wsl --update` instead, it is a much easier way to update your WSL kernel.
I will note that if you're using `wsl-vpnkit` and you run `wsl --update` without updating the vpnkit, you'll get a series of errors. Be sure to update that first!
Does any one resolved the issue?
